title: B-NLS: Burgers bore in u colliding with NLS bright soliton in N
ic: u(x,0) = 1.0 * (1 - tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore, u_L=1, u_R=0). N(x,0) = sech^2(x+8) (soliton at x=-8). phi(x,0) = 0.6*x (so phi_x = 0.6 — soliton moves toward bore).
ground_truth: Unknown. The interaction could be transmission, reflection, or capture. Use to characterize which methods handle the shock-soliton coupling.
stress_question: Bore in u creates a steep gradient. Pure spectral on u will Gibbs-oscillate. Try MUSCL-Godunov on u + Madelung-Psi on (N, phi). Does this split work? At what time does the soliton encounter the bore?
parameters_to_sweep: Method combinations: (1) all-spectral all-RK4 [will fail at bore], (2) MUSCL-Godunov u + spectral (N, phi) RK4, (3) MUSCL-Godunov u + Madelung-Psi (N, phi).
